findings.title: Unsafe port number parsing with Number() allows NaN to reach server.listen
findings.detail: server.ts:58 and daemon.ts:79 used Number(env['CONTROL_PLANE_PORT'] ?? default) without validation. An invalid env value like 'abc' silently produced NaN, causing server.listen(NaN) to fail unpredictably at runtime rather than at startup.
findings.fix_sketch: Replaced Number() with parseInt(str, 10) guarded by Number.isFinite() range check. Throws at startup if out of valid port range.

findings.title: Unsafe tick interval parsing with Number() allows NaN in setInterval
findings.detail: daemon.ts:537 used Number(process.env['IFLEET_DAEMON_TICK_MS'] ?? DEFAULT_TICK_MS) without validation. NaN passed to setInterval() causes Node.js to treat it as 1ms, creating a hot-loop that consumes 100% CPU.
findings.fix_sketch: Replaced Number() with parseInt(str, 10) with a minimum 100ms guard. Throws at startup on invalid value.

findings.title: Unsafe max-attempts parsing with Number() silently disables retry cap
findings.detail: store.ts:263 used Number(process.env['IFLEET_MAX_ATTEMPTS'] ?? 5) without validation. If the env var was set to a non-numeric value, maxAttempts became NaN and attempts >= NaN is always false in both JS and SQLite, so the retry cap never fired.
findings.fix_sketch: Replaced Number() with parseInt(str, 10) with isFinite validation. Falls back to default 5 with a console.warn on invalid value (does not throw, since recoverStale is non-critical path).

findings.title: Signal handler shutdown promise rejection is unhandled
findings.detail: server.ts:156-157 used void shutdown().then(() => process.exit(0)). If shutdown() rejected, the rejection was swallowed and process.exit(0) was never called, leaving the process hanging indefinitely on SIGTERM/SIGINT.
findings.fix_sketch: Added .catch() handlers that log the error and call process.exit(1) to ensure the process always terminates on signal.
